mnemonics: F.O.K.E. (Frame, Ontology, Knowledge, Epistemology) - The building blocks for representing domain knowledge.
keyPoints: Knowledge representation (KR) involves designing computer-interpretable structures for symbolically representing knowledge about a domain.
Ontologies provide a formal specification of a shared conceptualization, defining concepts, properties, and relationships.
Frames and semantic networks are common KR structures that organize knowledge in a hierarchical, interconnected way.
commonMistakes: Confusing an ontology (a formal model of concepts) with a simple taxonomy (a hierarchy of terms).
Overlooking the importance of choosing the right KR scheme for the specific problem domain and its intended use (reasoning).
